# Paper 3 — per-claim evidence graph (schema v2)

One row per registry gate wiring (a gate may split into several claim links). `evidence_strength` is the **per-gate adjudicated** tier; `component_evidence_strength` is the component's own declared tier (context only). `relationship` is the honest fit/evaluation relationship. `NEEDS_ADJUDICATION` rows are drafts — their verdicts are deliberately blank until curated.

**49 links total — 16 adjudicated, 33 awaiting adjudication.**

## Adjudicated claims

| link | owner | p3 use | support | tier | relationship | observable | claim |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `brewer2026.coupled_kappa_t::gate_kappa_t_composition_diagnostic` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | exploratory_synthesis | post_fit_same_data | Minimum composite porosity (eps_min vs eps0) and the 9-bar Q(t) RMSE vs the measured Waszkiewicz trace (g/s). | Adding the parameter-free mo2023_2 swelling branch behaves as the framework predicts: the composite porosity closes below eps0 and the 9-bar Q(t) residual jumps from ~0.12 to ~0.65 g/s (worse than the flat null ~0.603), diagnosing that mo2023_2's fixed-dP fresh-grain swelling is mis-scaled for an already-swollen saturated pre-wet rig. |
| `brewer2026.coupled_kappa_t::gate_kappa_t_degeneracy::reconstruction` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Mass flow Q(t) at 9 bar vs the measured Waszkiewicz trace over the 15-95 s saturated window (RMSE, g/s). | In that extraction-only reduction the coupled model's predicted 9-bar Q(t) reproduces the measured Waszkiewicz 9-bar flow ramp with RMSE ~0.113 g/s (gate <0.13), matching the poroelastic component's own reconstruction (rung 4). |
| `brewer2026.coupled_kappa_t::gate_kappa_t_degeneracy::reduction` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Structural identity of the coupled model's extraction-only Phi(t)/Q(t) closure to the standalone poroelastic component (a code property). | With the swelling/compaction/fines-loss branches disabled, the coupled shared-porosity synthesis reduces exactly to its degenerate special case waszkiewicz2025.poroelastic (extraction-only Phi(t)=m_d(t)/m0 through the same poroelastic closure), adding no new behaviour in this limit. |
| `brewer2026.lb_reference::gate_lb_channel` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Darcy permeability k of a plane-Poiseuille channel in lattice units vs the closed-form analytic value h^2/12. | The D3Q19 TRT lattice-Boltzmann permeability kernel reproduces the exact plane-Poiseuille channel permeability h^2/12 to within 0.5% (reported +0.03%). |
| `cameron2020.extraction_bdf::gate_cameron_conservation` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Extraction yield EY (%) via two internal accounting routes plus an inventory upper bound, all computed from the simulation and module constants. | The cameron2020 BDF two-population solver conserves solute mass (cup-mass EY agrees with solid-depletion EY to within 0.5 pp) and the predicted EY stays strictly below the model's own per-bed soluble-inventory ceiling. |
| `foster2025.infiltration::gate_infiltration_triangle` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | sign_or_compatibility | same_campaign_not_held_out | First-liquid / bed-saturation time (s): observed = time cup weight first exceeds 0.5 g; predicted = t_saturate when the modelled front reaches bed depth L, at porosity endpoints 0.173 and 0.322. | A sharp-front infiltration prediction, driven by the DE1 shot's own measured pressure trace and a permeability from that shot's fitted kappa, yields a bed-saturation-time window (across an a-priori porosity bracket 0.173-0.322) that brackets the observed first-drip time (~7 s). |
| `foster2025.machine_mode::gate_foster_machine_tp_ts` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Two scalar times (s): ponding time t_p and saturation time t_s, model-computed vs the authors' reported values. | The staged pump/headspace machine-mode ODE, run from the transcribed Table I/II parameters and offset by the fitted t_shift=0.796 s, reproduces the authors' reported ponding time t_p=0.823 s and saturation time t_s=6.669 s. |
| `liang2021.desorption::gate_liang_kemax_refit` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | The dimensionless equilibrium desorption product K*E_max from equilibrium TDS vs brew ratio R_brew for full-immersion brewing. | Re-fitting the lumped equilibrium product K*E_max via Eq. 11 to the digitized Fig. 3 TDS-vs-brew-ratio data (R_brew>=3) recovers ~0.215, the card's reported value. |
| `mo2023_2.swelling::gate_mo2_swelling_insensitivity` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | End-of-shot yield (fraction of extractable inventory) with swelling on vs off at q=2/3/4 mL/s (fixed-q), contrasted with the fixed-dP Carman-Kozeny flow-decay ratio. | At fixed flow the implemented mo2023_2 two-population model gives nearly identical yield with swelling on vs off (max relative difference <5%) because swelling raises grain porosity/D_p (~+15%) but grows R^2 (~+7%) and these offset — reproducing the paper's Fig. 2 model behaviour. |
| `pannusch2024.solver::gate_pannusch_solver_mape` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Per-solute MAPE (%) between simulated and measured fraction concentrations over 15 experiments x 6 time-fraction windows. | Run with the authors' Table 2 per-solute fitted parameters, the two-grain multi-solute PDE solver reproduces the authors' own published fit MAPEs against the Schmieder kinetics (TDS 6.07, caffeine 4.59, trigonelline 7.85, CGA 4.98 %, each within ~3.5 pts). |
| `romancorrochano2017.extraction::gate_roman_bed_mpe_parameter_free` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | source_curve_reproduction | same_campaign_not_held_out | Mean percentage error (MPE, %) between the thesis's predicted and measured espresso yields per molecular-weight Deff class, digitized from Fig. 7.4 (~+/-0.5 pp). | Across all 15 digitized Fig. 7.4 espresso conditions the thesis's reported medium-MW (non-fitted microstructural Deff) bed-scale prediction error is MPE <=14.3%, and the medium-MW single-Deff column has a lower mean MPE than the low- or high-MW columns. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_intersource_spread` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | independent_external | Per-cell percent offset (mu_khomyakov - mu_TR2001)/mu_TR2001 over the overlapping concentration/temperature cells, summarized by median/min/max and sign. | Two independent measured coffee-extract viscosity datasets (khomyakov2020 kinematic converted to dynamic mu=rho*nu vs telisromero2001 Eq-10 closure) agree in sign/direction across their Xw 76-90% / T 295-365 K overlap but khomyakov runs systematically ~+37% (median 25-50%, max <60%) above TR2001. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_reference_mu_above_water` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | not_empirical | Reference espresso dynamic-viscosity ratio to pure water at brew temperature, plus Newtonian-band membership. | The transcribed reference espresso viscosity estimate (~4-6e-4 Pa.s, ~1.3-1.9x pure water 3.15e-4 Pa.s) exceeds pure water, stays within 1.0-2.5x, and lies in the Newtonian band (espresso 4-12% solids is below the ~36%-solids power-law onset). |
| `wadsworth2026.permeability::gate_wadsworth_collapse` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Dimensionless collapse ratio k_measured / (k_star(phi_p,s_p)*phi_p^4.4) across the Table 1 grind rows, aggregated as a geometric mean. | The paper's percolation permeability form k = k_star(phi_p,s_p)*phi_p^4.4 collapses their own Table 1 measured permeabilities to a geometric-mean ratio near 0.9 (gate window 0.7 < GM < 1.2). |
| `waszkiewicz2025.poroelastic::gate_waszkiewicz_dynamic_9bar` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Mass flow Q(t) at 9 bar vs time (long-run mean g/s and Pearson correlation, t>=15 s). | With zero extra free parameters (static (P_c,Q_c) plus the dissolution sigmoid Phi(t)=m_d(t)/m0), Eq. 18 reproduces the measured 9-bar Q(t) ramp semi-quantitatively — long-run mean within 10% and correlation >0.90 over t>=15 s. |
| `waszkiewicz2025.poroelastic::gate_waszkiewicz_static_refit` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Steady-state basket-pressure vs mass-flow curve; recovered calibration parameters P_c, Q_c. | Refitting the poroelastic equilibrium closure (Eq. 16) to the 11-pressure long-run pressure-flow curve recovers the calibration pair (P_c,Q_c) ~ (12 bar, 1.90 g/s) and reproduces the paper's ingested static calibration to <0.05 bar / <0.01 g/s. |
